title: Array Index Out of Bounds Bug Fix

statement.markdown:
Given an array of integers, find the first pair of elements that add up to a given target value. If such a pair is found, return the indices of the two elements. Otherwise, return an empty list.

Note that the input array's elements are distinct and the target value is an integer.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided in the required format (stdin/stdin). You will receive the size of the array, then the array elements, followed by the target value.
statement.outputFormat: Your function should return a list of two integers, representing the indices of the two elements that add up to the target value. If no such pair is found, return an empty list.
statement.notes: Your solution should handle the case where multiple pairs of elements add up to the target value.

examples.explanation: No pair of elements adds up to 10.

hints.text: Use a two-pointer approach to find the pair of elements that add up to the target value.

editorial.title: Two-Pointer Approach
editorial.intuition: We can use two pointers, one at the start of the array and one at the end. We then compare the sum of the elements at these two pointers with the target value. If the sum is less than the target, we move the left pointer to the right. If the sum is greater than the target, we move the right pointer to the left.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a brute-force approach by iterating over each element in the array and checking if the difference between the target value and the current element is in the array.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a two-pointer approach to find the pair of elements that add up to the target value.
editorial.optimalSolution: The two-pointer approach has a time complexity of O(n), making it more efficient than the brute-force approach.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The two-pointer approach is correct because it covers all possible pairs of elements in the array.
editorial.dryRun: To dry-run the solution, we can use the examples provided in the input format section.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where multiple pairs of elements add up to the target value.
Not checking if the target value is achievable with the given array elements.

class Solution:

def find_pair(self, arr, target, result):

functionSignature.cpp: bool findPair(int n, int arr[], int target, int &i, int &j);
functionSignature.java: boolean findPair(int n, int arr[], int target, int[] result);
functionSignature.python: def find_pair(self, arr, target, result: list): bool;
